name: Coffee-Almond Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 1 cup sugar in a small saucepan over low heat and cook until dark brown and caramelized.
2. Quickly pour into the heated 9x2", 6-cup round non-aluminum pan (equal in diameter to the size of the springform pan used) and swirl until bottom and sides are evenly coated with caramel.
3. For custard: Preheat oven to 325°F. Scald espresso and whipping cream in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat. Remove from heat and let cool. Combine 6 eggs and 1/2 cup sugar in a large bowl and beat well. Stir in the espresso mixture and 6 tablespoons cocoa. Pour into the prepared pan. Cover with a round of buttered wax paper and set into a larger pan. Add enough boiling water to come halfway up the sides of the pan. Bake custard until set, about 1 1/2 hours. Remove from water; let cool slightly and refrigerate.
4. For cake: Preheat oven to 325°F. Generously butter and flour a 9-inch springform pan, shaking out excess. Cream butter with sugar in a large bowl until light and fluffy. Beat in egg yolks, blending thoroughly. Gradually stir in flour, cookie crumbs, and 2 teaspoons cocoa. Beat egg whites in a medium bowl until stiff peaks form. Gently fold into batter. Transfer to prepared pan and bake about 20 minutes (cake should be slightly soft in the center). Let cool.
5. To assemble: Remove sides of springform pan and loosen cake from bottom. Run a sharp, thin knife around custard and dip the bottom of the pan briefly in hot water to loosen if necessary. Place cake atop custard. Center serving platter on cake. Place one hand on the bottom of the mold and the other hand on top of the platter. Grasp firmly and invert. Carefully remove mold.
6. Garnish top of custard with additional crushed amaretti, if desired.
7. Serve immediately. You can substitute strong coffee.
